headline: 37 contracts catalogued (17 L1 OP Stack + 15 L2 predeploys + 4 Base-Solana bridge + 1 audit anchor); 7 admin roles enumerated; Security Council (3-of-13 SystemConfig + 9-of-12 upgrade quorum) verified on-chain.

findings.text: Block-explorer verification: DisputeGameFactoryProxy 0x43edB88C4B80fDD2AdFF2412A7BebF9dF42cB40e fetched directly on Etherscan, confirming it is a Proxy receiving hourly Output Proposer (0x642229f2...) Initialize calls that create FaultDisputeGame instances.

Official deployments doc walked at https://docs.base.org/base-chain/network-information/base-contracts (17 L1 contracts + 15 L2 predeploys + 7 L1 admin roles for Base Mainnet) and https://docs.base.org/base-chain/quickstart/base-solana-bridge (4 Base-Mainnet contracts for Base-Solana bridge). docs_url and deployed_contracts_doc populated.
Audit PDFs: github.com/base/bridge contains an /audits folder confirmed at the repo root listing, but individual audit reports could not be enumerated within fetch budget (see unknowns).

GitHub deployment artifacts: base/bridge repo (https://github.com/base/bridge) confirmed as canonical source for Base-Solana bridge with releases tagged through bridge/v1.0.0 (Dec 2025); deployment artifact JSON not enumerated this run.

Multi-chain enumeration: Base bridge spans Ethereum L1 (canonical OP Stack contracts) and Base L2 (predeploys at 0x4200…). Each chain enumerated separately with chainId-prefixed admin_addresses[] entries.

Factory-discovered children: DisputeGameFactoryProxy (0x43edB88C…dF42cB40e) is a live factory creating FaultDisputeGame instances roughly hourly (Etherscan shows 11,745 transactions); per-game children intentionally not enumerated — record factory and cap (see unknowns for D6 cap notice).
Role taxonomy applied: proxy_admin / multisig / guardian / token / bridge / pool / factory / oracle / other used per controlled vocabulary; multisig threshold info recorded where verified on-chain.

Ratchet integrity: every address in admin_addresses[] traces to ≥1 fetched URL (docs.base.org base-contracts page, Etherscan, DeFiPunkd surfacer, security-council page, or base-solana-bridge page). Pinned address-book entry 0x1453E056E8E3…E3E3 is recorded as suspected-fabricated in unknowns rather than echoed forward.

rationale.verdict: Discovery is non-judgmental; corpora walked: docs.base.org official Base-Contracts page (D2 primary), docs.base.org Base-Solana-Bridge page (D2 secondary), docs.base.org Security Council page (member EOAs), Etherscan (D1 verification of one contested address), DeFiPunkd surfacers (D2 multisig threshold/owner verification on three Safes), github.com/base/bridge (D4 source repo).

evidence.shows: Canonical Base deployed-contracts page listing 17 L1 contracts (AddressManager, AnchorStateRegistryProxy, DelayedWETHProxy FDG/PDG, DisputeGameFactoryProxy 0x43edB88C…dF42cB40e, FaultDisputeGame, L1CrossDomainMessenger, L1ERC721Bridge, L1StandardBridge, MIPS, OptimismMintableERC20Factory, OptimismPortal, PermissionedDisputeGame, PreimageOracle, ProxyAdmin, SystemConfig, SystemDictator), 15 L2 predeploys (0x4200…), and 7 Base Mainnet admin roles (Batch Sender, Batch Inbox, Output Proposer, Proxy Admin Owner Safe, Challenger, SystemConfig owner Safe, Guardian Safe).

Base canonical bridges entry page (redirects to /bridges); references bridge.base.org as deprecated, lists Superbridge / Brid.gg / Garden / Base-Solana paths and embeds Base-Solana contract addresses (Base Bridge 0x3eff766C…, SOL token 0x311935Cd…).

Base-Solana Bridge contract addresses block: Base Mainnet { Bridge 0x3eff766C76a1be2Ce1aCF2B69c78bCae257D5188, BridgeValidator 0xAF24c1c24Ff3BF1e6D882518120fC25442d6794B, CrossChainERC20Factory 0xDD56781d0509650f8C2981231B6C917f2d5d7dF2, SOL 0x311935Cd80B76769bF2ecC9D8Ab7635b2139cf82 }; Solana Mainnet { BridgeProgram, BaseRelayerProgram }. Architecture: validators verify Merkle roots ~every 300 finalized blocks.

Base Security Council composition: 12 entities (11 independent + Coinbase) with ≥75% (9/12) quorum required for upgrades. Lists 10 published EOA signer addresses for Aerodrome, Moonwell, Blackbird, ChainSafe, Talent Protocol, Moshicam, Seneca, Juan Suarez, Toady Hawk, Roberto Bayardo. Two cohorts: term ends Oct 2026 / Jan 2027.
Bug bounty program: Coinbase HackerOne extended to cover Base network, Base bridge contracts, and Base infrastructure; runs alongside Optimism's Immunefi Bedrock bounty.

Proxy Admin Owner (L1) is a Gnosis Safe v1.3.0, threshold 2-of-2, owners [0x9855054731540A48b28990B63DcF4f33d8AE46A1, 0x20AcF55A3DCfe07fC4cecaCFa1628F788EC8A4Dd]. Pinned to block 25024122. Implementation proxy 0xd9Db270c1B5E3Bd161E8c8503c55cEABeE709552 (canonical Safe singleton).
Guardian is a Gnosis Safe v1.3.0, threshold 1-of-1, single owner [0xc2819DC788505Aac350142A7A707BF9D03E3Bd03]. Pinned to block 25024123.

SystemConfig owner is a Gnosis Safe v1.3.0, threshold 3-of-13, with 13 owner EOAs. Pinned to block 25024125. Nonce 105 (heavy operational use). Owners include addresses associated with the Security Council roster published on docs.base.org/base-chain/security/security-council.

DisputeGameFactoryProxy at 0x43edB88C…dF42cB40e (note: dF, NOT dD as in pinned address_book) is a verified Proxy contract (creator 0x4B7FcC44…7E38e23E5, deployed ~1 yr 148 days ago) with 11,745 total transactions; receives hourly 'Initialize' / 0x6101583d (Create) calls from Output Proposer 0x642229f238fb9de03374be34b0ed8d9de80752c5 that spawn new FaultDisputeGame children. Implementation 0xc040F392…4E07C5e2c.

Surfacer for the address as pinned in address_book reports 'No verified ABI found … etherscan + sourcify both failed' — confirming the pinned address (with dD) does NOT correspond to a verified contract on Ethereum mainnet, supporting the conclusion that it is a 1-character typo of the actual DisputeGameFactoryProxy address.

Canonical GitHub repo for the Base-Solana bridge (Coinbase / base GitHub org); MIT licensed; 426 commits; latest release bridge/v1.0.0 (Dec 5, 2025); structure includes /audits, /base (Solidity), /solana (Rust), /scripts, /clients/ts. Languages: TypeScript 50.5%, Rust 28.5%, Solidity 20.5%.

unknowns: D3: github.com/base/bridge has an /audits folder but individual report PDFs (firm names, dates, scope tables) could not be enumerated within fetch budget — next run should fetch https://github.com/base/bridge/tree/main/audits and per-file URLs.

D3-canonical-OP: audits for the canonical OP Stack contracts (OptimismPortal, L1StandardBridge, FaultDisputeGame system) deployed on L1 are inherited from Optimism's audit history (Sherlock, Spearbit, Cantina) but no Base-specific audit URL was fetched this run; these should be located via the ethereum-optimism/optimism repo audits/ directory or Optimism docs.

D4: Foundry broadcast/ or hardhat-deploy deployments/ artifacts inside github.com/base/bridge were not enumerated this run; canonical OP Stack contract deployment artifacts live in github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism (not base/bridge) and were not fetched.

D6: DisputeGameFactoryProxy is a high-throughput factory creating ~24 FaultDisputeGame children per day; per-game enumeration intentionally skipped (cap-by-design) — children are short-lived bond contracts, not control-plane addresses, so they are correctly outside the discovery surface.

D5: zero addresses surfaced for Base testnet (Sepolia) admin Safes from on-chain verification — testnet is documented on docs.base.org/base-chain/network-information/base-contracts but not material for this mainnet-focused assessment.

D8: pinned address_book entry 0x1453E056E8E3E3E3E3E3E3E3E3E3E3E3E3E3E3E3 ('Base Multisig 1 — Incident Responder, 3/13') exhibits a clearly synthetic 'E3'-repeating pattern in its lower 14 bytes and could not be located on Etherscan or in docs.base.org/base-chain/network-information/base-contracts; the actual 3-of-13 SystemConfig owner Safe is 0x14536667Cd30e52C0b458BaACcB9faDA7046E056 (already pinned) — the E3-padded entry should be removed in a future ratchet pass.

D8: pinned address_book entry 0x43edB88C4B80fDD2AdFF2412A7BebF9dD42cB40e (DisputeGameFactoryProxy) is a 1-char typo of the actual deployed DisputeGameFactoryProxy 0x43edB88C4B80fDD2AdFF2412A7BebF9dF42cB40e (dD vs dF in the 14th hex pair); next ratchet pass should correct this so downstream slices fetch live state instead of an empty contract.

D2: Solana side of the Base-Solana bridge (BridgeProgram HNCne2FkVaNghhjKXapxJzPaBvAKDG1Ge3gqhZyfVWLM, BaseRelayerProgram g1et5VenhfJHJwsdJsDbxWZuotD5H4iELNG61kS4fb9) cannot be expressed in Ethereum-style admin_addresses[] (no chainId for Solana); recorded in 'about' but not in admin_addresses.

admin_addresses.role: other (OptimismPortal — main L1 deposit/withdrawal entry; ETH escrow)
factory (DisputeGameFactoryProxy — creates FaultDisputeGame instances; corrected from typo in pinned address_book)
other (PreimageOracle — preimage oracle for fault proofs)
proxy_admin (L1 ProxyAdmin — owns most L1 OP-Stack proxies)
owner (Proxy Admin Owner — owns L1 ProxyAdmin; 2-of-2 Safe v1.3.0)
other (sub-owner of Proxy Admin Owner Safe)
other (sub-owner of Proxy Admin Owner Safe)
other (Output Proposer — Coinbase-managed EOA; creates FaultDisputeGames hourly)
other (Batch Inbox — EOA with no known private key; 0xff…+chainId 8453)
proxy_admin (L2 ProxyAdmin predeploy — owns L2 predeploy proxies)
other (Base-Solana Bridge contract — locks/burns SOL/SPL tokens, builds Merkle trees)
other (Base-Solana BridgeValidator — validates oracle signatures of Solana → Base messages)

protocol_metadata.about: The Base Bridge is the canonical, OP-Stack-based bridging surface between Ethereum and Base, Coinbase's L2 rollup. Users deposit ETH and ERC-20s on Ethereum (L1StandardBridge / OptimismPortal) and receive wrapped balances on Base via L2 predeploys at 0x4200…0010 / 0007; withdrawals require posting an output root to the DisputeGameFactoryProxy and waiting for the FaultDisputeGame challenge window to expire before claiming through the OptimismPortal. As of April 2025 the bridge's L1 contracts are upgradeable through a 9-of-12 Security Council quorum (11 independent entities + Coinbase) plus the 2-of-2 Proxy Admin Owner Safe, with a 1-of-1 Guardian Safe authorized to pause withdrawals and a 3-of-13 SystemConfig owner Safe handling chain-parameter operations. A separate Base-Solana bridge (deployed in /base/bridge GitHub repo, contracts on Base Mainnet and Solana Mainnet-Beta) uses an oracle-validator architecture that posts Merkle roots roughly every 300 finalized Base blocks.
